notes: Lane et al. 2024, BMJ umbrella review; 45 pooled analyses, 9.9M participants; UPFs linked to 71% of 45 health parameters; convincing evidence: CVD mortality (RR 1.50), T2D (RR 1.12), anxiety (RR 1.48), common mental disorders (RR 1.53)
News article, not peer-reviewed; advocacy framing; cigarette analogy is provocative but the underlying claim has primary research sources
Liang et al. 2025, updated dose-response meta-analysis; 18 studies, 1.1M participants, 173K deaths; highest vs lowest UPF: HR=1.15 all-cause mortality; each 10% increment: HR=1.10
Mendoza et al. 2024, Lancet Regional Health Americas; NHS + NHSII + HPFS cohorts (207K participants, 16800 CVD events) + meta-analysis; significant UPF-CVD, CHD, stroke associations
